title: PEPFAR-focused HIV infrastructure and non-communicable disease trial capacity in Africa
body: Does PEPFAR-built HIV clinical trial infrastructure transfer to non-communicable disease research capacity in recipient African countries? We queried ClinicalTrials.gov for interventional HIV, NCD, and total trials across ten PEPFAR-focus countries receiving over one hundred million dollars annually, comparing them with eight non-PEPFAR African nations of similar income level. The PEPFAR Spillover Index, defined as NCD trials divided by HIV trials, was computed alongside per-capita trial rates and HIV Dominance Scores for each individual country. PEPFAR-focus countries had high HIV trial density but spillover indices generally below one, indicating that NCD trial activity remained much lower than HIV activity in those nations. Comparator non-PEPFAR countries often had fewer total trials but exhibited more balanced portfolios across disease areas with lower HIV dominance scores. The pattern suggests that disease-specific infrastructure investment has not automatically translated into proportional NCD research capacity in Africa. Ecological comparisons cannot establish causation, and the analysis may miss African-led studies not captured in ClinicalTrials.gov.

sentences.text: Does large HIV research investment appear to spill over into broader non-communicable disease trial capacity in high-burden African countries?
We queried ClinicalTrials.gov for interventional HIV trials, NCD trials, and total trials across ten major PEPFAR-focus countries and eight non-PEPFAR comparators with similar income levels.
The primary metric was the PEPFAR Spillover Index, defined as NCD trials divided by HIV trials, alongside per-capita trial density and HIV dominance scores.
PEPFAR-focus countries had high HIV trial density but spillover indices generally below 1, indicating that NCD trial activity remained much lower than HIV activity.
Comparator countries often had fewer total trials but more balanced portfolios across disease areas.
The pattern suggests that disease-specific infrastructure has not automatically translated into proportional NCD research capacity.
Ecological comparisons cannot identify causation and may miss African-led studies not captured in ClinicalTrials.gov.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 10 PEPFAR-focus + 8 non-PEPFAR African countries

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.
